title: Canadian food prices relative to disposable income
description: Monthly consumer-price observations are averaged within completed quarters and compared with quarterly disposable income per person. No monthly income values are invented.
plainLanguageSummary: Monthly consumer-price observations are averaged within completed quarters and compared with quarterly disposable income per person. No monthly income values are invented.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Published indices and explicitly labelled derived changes; no interpolation across native frequencies.
calculation.explanation: Monthly consumer-price observations are averaged within completed quarters and compared with quarterly disposable income per person. No monthly income values are invented.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: Monthly consumer-price observations are averaged within completed quarters and compared with quarterly disposable income per person. No monthly income values are invented.
